UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, settings, and preferences within the SpendSense application. This entity stores information necessary for personalized experience and notification delivery, assuming authentication is handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically comes from the external authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, used for sending alerts and monthly reports. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 ExternalAuth)

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code preferred by the user for financial tracking, e.g., 'USD', 'EUR'.

dailySpendingLimit.description: The maximum amount a user wishes to spend per day, for alert generation.

receiveDailyAlerts.description: Indicates whether the user wants to receive daily spending limit alerts via email.

receiveMonthlyReports.description: Indicates whether the user wants to receive monthly spending reports via email.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines a classification for expenses or income. Categories can be system-provided or custom-created by users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created this custom category. Null for system-provided default categories. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Category)

name.description: The name of the category, e.g., 'Groceries', 'Rent', 'Salary'.

description.description: A brief description of what this category represents.

type.description: The type of financial flow this category represents, e.g., 'expense', 'income'.

icon.description: A string representing an icon associated with the category for UI display (e.g., an icon name or SVG path).

color.description: A hexadecimal color code associated with the category for UI display, e.g., '#FF0000'.

isDefault.description: Flag indicating if this is a system-provided default category (true) or a user-defined custom category (false).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category was last updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Records individual financial transactions, specifically outgoing expenditures. This is central to tracking and visualization features.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who recorded this expense. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this expense belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Expense)

amount.description: The monetary value of the expense.

description.description: A brief description or merchant name for the expense.

transactionDate.description: The date and time when the expense occurred.

note.description: Optional additional details or notes about the expense.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the expense record was last updated.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a user's monthly budget goal for a specific category, enabling tracking of spending against targets.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who set this budget. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Budget)

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this budget applies to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Budget)

period.description: The month and year for which the budget is set, e.g., '2023-11' for November 2023.

budgetAmount.description: The target monetary amount for the budget in this category for the specified period.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the budget record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the budget record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Each document ID is the user's unique authentication ID (`userId`). This path ensures explicit ownership and direct access for the authenticated user to their profile settings.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: User-specific categories for expenses and income. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Default categories (isDefault: true) are also stored here, specifically for the user, allowing for personal customization or referencing system-wide defaults uniquely for each user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, identifying the owner of the categories.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific category.

definition.description: Records individual expenses made by a user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring only the owning user can access their expenses.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, identifying the owner of the expense.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific expense record.

definition.description: User-defined budget goals for specific categories and periods. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, ensuring only the owning user can access their budgets.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, identifying the owner of the budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific budget record.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for SpendSense prioritizes strong user data isolation, clear ownership, and simplified security rules by strictly adhering to path-based authorization and denormalization. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `users` collection, with each user having a dedicated document (`/users/{userId}`) and subcollections for their associated entities.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding the `userId` directly into the path for all user-owned data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/categories/{categoryId}`). This ensures that `request.auth.uid` can be directly compared against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, making the authorization context immediately available without requiring any `get()` operations on parent documents. For additional robustness and clarity, the `userId` field is also included within the `Category`, `Expense`, and `Budget` documents, creating a redundant ownership check that simplifies rule writing and improves data integrity. The `UserProfile` document itself is keyed by the user's UID (`/users/{userId}`), making its ownership explicit and atomic.

**Querying for Authorization Posture (QAPs):** The structural segregation mandate is fully applied. Each collection (`categories`, `expenses`, `budgets`) under a user's path (`/users/{userId}/...`) contains documents with a homogeneous security posture: they are all owned by and private to that specific user. This enables secure and efficient `list` operations. Firestore security rules can simply verify that `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the wildcard in the path). This guarantees that any `list` query from a client can only ever retrieve data belonging to the authenticated user, thus ensuring that rules are not used as filters and preventing accidental data exposure. For example, a query to `/users/my_uid/expenses` will only return expenses belonging to 'my_uid', without the need for additional `where` clauses in the rules.

This design pattern makes the Firestore security rules straightforward, declarative, and easy to debug, as authorization logic is primarily derived from the data's location within the hierarchy.
